channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Sen. Blumenthal applauds law that expands Social Security benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (WTNH) -- Nearly 3 million Americans could see a boost in Social Security payments, thanks to a bipartisan act awaiting President Joe Biden's signature.
item.dc:creator: Eva Zymaris, NewsNation

item.title: NYPD hunting for suspect after baby abandoned in bag on NYC street
item.description: Police are searching for a person they say left an infant girl in a tote bag Sunday morning in the Bronx.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Convicted South Carolina bank killer asks for 'compassionate release' days after Biden commuted death sentence
item.description: A convicted killer who ruthlessly gunned down two South Carolina bank employees in 2017 has demanded to be released from prison days after President Biden saved him from death row.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US this New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year. The sun expelled two bursts of plasma that are hurtling toward Earth and are expected to arrive early this week, according to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: NYPD operating in 'heightened threat environment' in advance of New Year's Eve celebration
item.description: The NYPD continues to operate under a "heightened threat environment" as it prepares for New Year's Eve festivities in Times Square.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border arrests are little changed in December, ending Biden's term at lowest level
item.description: A U.S. official says arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in December are little changed from a month earlier.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Keanu Reeves’ stolen watches located in Chile: reports
item.description: Stolen luxury watches belonging to Keanu Reeves, including a Rolex worth almost $10,000, have been found far from where they were stolen.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Elk with chair on head in Ruidoso is able to move, eat
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- An elk with a chair on its head is being reported in the Ruidoso area, but authorities say the animal has been evaluated and is doing OK. The Ruidoso Police Department said Saturday evening, Dec.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration's final aid to Ukraine before Donald Trump takes office is a $1.25 billion drawdown package.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives complete and total endorsement of Johnson as Speaker
item.description: President-elect Trump offered his complete and total endorsement of Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, days before a House vote on Friday to elect a new Speaker. "Speaker Mike Johnson is a good, hard working, religious man. He will do the right thing, and we will continue to WIN.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: An appeals court upholds a $5 million award in a sexual abuse verdict against President-elect Trump
item.description: A federal appeals court has upheld a jury's finding in a civil case that Donald Trump sexually abused a columnist in an upscale department store dressing room in the mid-1990s.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trader Joe's is opening a dozen new locations in 2025: Here's where
item.description: The company’s website currently lists 12 planned locations “opening soon” across seven states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Value-seekers drove 2024's retail trends and dead ends
item.description: Value was in vogue in 2024. Shoppers and restaurant patrons in the U.S. were choosy about where and how to spend their money as they wrestled with high housing and food prices. Well-heeled customers traded down to Walmart and Aldi.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stranded sea turtle rescued near Oceanside, Oregon
item.description: A stranded loggerhead sea turtle was rescued from the rocky shoreline of Short Beach in Tillamook County on Dec. 28.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: How US presidents have planned their own funerals
item.description: Funerals often are planned by the presidents themselves, who usually have years after leaving the White House to ponder how they want to be memorialized.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Jimmy Carter's marriage to Rosalynn Carter helped shape his legacy
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter’s marriage to first lady Rosalynn Carter played a defining role in his life, spanning 77 years and making it the longest presidential marriage in U.S. history. Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WGN 9

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100
item.description: Former President Carter, the unassuming Georgia peanut farmer whose improbable political rise took him to the governor’s mansion before the White House, died peacefully on Sunday in Plains, Ga., according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Scott Wong, ABC 4

item.title: Tornadoes in Texas and Mississippi kill 2 and injure 6 as severe weather system moves east
item.description: A strong storm system threatened to whip up tornadoes in parts of the U.S. Southeast on Sunday, a day after severe weather claimed at least two lives as twisters touched down in Texas and Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: ABC 4

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KRON4

item.title: Several injured after multi-vehicle crash in California city involving police vehicles
item.description: The Oakland Police Department (OPD) is investigating a crash involving two Bay Area Rapid Transit (BART) Police Department vehicles on Saturday and injured "several" people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 15 hurt in Florida when train hits fire truck that drove onto tracks after another train passed
item.description: Three firefighters and a dozen passengers were injured in Florida on Saturday when a fire truck with its lights flashing drove around rail crossing arms and into the path of a high-speed passenger train after waiting for another train to pass, according to video of the incident and a person briefed
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These are America's safest small towns: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LDS mission president from Utah dies 'unexpectedly' in France
item.description: A mission president for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints died on Saturday, Dec. 28 in France, according to a spokesperson for the church.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: LIST: Top 10 wackiest Florida Man (and Woman) headlines of 2024
item.description: Another year has come and gone, but the wacky antics of Florida residents will live on forever through the internet.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Heading into 2024, we said the U.S. economy would likely continue growing, in spite of pundits’ forecast that a recession would strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KRON4

item.title: Southern California community among safest small town in America: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Abortions are up in the US. It's a complicated picture as women turn to pills, travel
item.description: Abortion has become slightly more common despite bans or deep restrictions in most Republican-controlled states, and the legal and political fights over its future are not over yet. It's now been two and a half years since the U.S. Supreme Court overturned Roe v.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and KEVIN S. VINEYS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open. Big Lots said Friday it will be sold to Gordon Brothers Retail Partners, a firm that specializes in distressed companies.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina McDonald's employee fatally shoots DoorDash driver, police say
item.description: A McDonald's employee who was on the loose after a shooting that killed a DoorDash delivery driver Thursday night has been taken into custody.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: California residents on edge as high surf and flooding threats persist on Christmas Eve
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — Persistent high surf and flooding threats along California’s coast had residents on high alert a day after a major storm was blamed for one man’s death and the partial collapse of a pier, which propelled three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4
